summary: Machine learning engineer with hands-on experience training deep learning models, building containerized inference infrastructure, and designing evaluation pipelines that measure model performance systematically. Experienced shipping full-stack AI systems end to end, from data augmentation and model training through deployment on cloud infrastructure with observability into results. Motivated by autonomous AI systems and recursive improvement loops, with a track record of using experiment outcomes to drive the next iteration.

skills.ml: PyTorch, YOLO, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, LangChain, RAG, fine-tuning, ensemble modeling, SHAP, Captum, OpenAI API, Claude API, Deepgram
skills.infra: Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions, Railway, GCP, Supabase (PostgreSQL), CI/CD, horizontal scaling, experiment tracking, REST APIs

experience.title: Deep Learning Intern
bullets: Built a data augmentation pipeline using NumPy and Pandas, scaling the training dataset from a few hundred to 10,000+ images and manually re-annotating 500+ edge cases; used held-out evaluation data to measure accuracy improvement from 65% to 85.4%, treating each augmentation pass as an experiment that informed the next.

Developed a YOLO-based object detection model in Python to detect and classify industrial pipe inventory from warehouse images, improving accuracy by 30% and eliminating a fully manual counting process.
Containerized the model with Docker and deployed on Kubernetes for horizontal scaling of model inference, enabling the team to handle variable load without code changes.
Designed normalized relational database schemas in SQL to store image metadata and inference results, enabling cross-team queries on model performance and inventory trends.

projects.name: AI Interview Engine
projects.subtitle: Voice-First Candidate Screening Platform
projects.stack: Next.js, Express, Claude Sonnet/Haiku, Deepgram, Supabase, Railway, Vercel
bullets: Built an automated post-interview evaluation system that scores candidates across 4 dimensions and produces advance/hold/reject output, creating a structured feedback loop where each session produces a measurable result against a consistent rubric.

Designed a multi-turn conversational AI engine with dynamic context injection: the LLM receives each candidate's parsed resume before the session and generates targeted, role-specific follow-up questions in real time, demonstrating LLM-driven planning over a combinatorial space of possible questions.

Engineered a real-time voice pipeline integrating Deepgram Nova-2 (speech-to-text) and Aura (text-to-speech) with Claude Sonnet 4.6, achieving 4-5s end-to-end latency per conversational turn and replacing human interviewers for initial candidate screening.

Built an automated document ingestion pipeline (PDF parsing, Claude Haiku, MD5 deduplication caching) and deployed the full stack on Railway, Vercel, and Supabase (PostgreSQL, Row-Level Security, Magic Link authentication) with Brevo SMTP for transactional email automation.

projects.name: CodeSyntax AI
projects.subtitle: Full-Stack RAG Developer Assistant
projects.stack: Python, FastAPI, Next.js, LangChain, Pinecone, Docker, GitHub Actions
bullets: Maintained a 55-question domain evaluation set alongside 35+ pytest unit tests, enabling systematic measurement of retrieval and generation quality after every code change and using results to guide improvements to ranking and retrieval logic.

Built a production retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) system using GPT-4o-mini with semantic search over Python Docs, RealPython, and StackOverflow, indexed in Pinecone via OpenAI vector embeddings.

Implemented an LLM-based query intent classifier and result re-ranker by source authority, plus multi-turn conversation support via LangChain chat-history condensing, both measurably reducing off-topic responses.

Maintained a GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline that builds the Docker image and deploys to Railway (API) and Vercel (frontend) on every merge to main, keeping infrastructure reproducible and experiment iterations fast.

projects.name: AuthentiText
projects.subtitle: AI-Generated Text Detection Engine
projects.stack: Python, XGBoost, DistilBERT, HuggingFace, GCP, Docker
bullets: Conducted systematic model comparison across four architectures (Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, DistilBERT) using ROC curves, confusion matrices, and SHAP feature importance, treating each architecture as a hypothesis evaluated against the same held-out data before selecting the best combination.

Trained a weighted ensemble model combining XGBoost (21 hand-crafted linguistic features: perplexity, burstiness, lexical density) with a fine-tuned DistilBERT transformer, achieving 99.1% F1 and 99.96% AUROC on held-out test data.

Trained on the merged HC3 corpus spanning Wikipedia, Reddit, medicine, and finance domains; integrated Captum token-level attribution heatmaps for explainable AI (XAI); deployed as a publicly accessible machine learning inference app on HuggingFace Spaces.
